title: Blechnum banksii
taxonomy: Blechnum banksii is a species of fern in the family Blechnaceae. It belongs to the genus Blechnum, which includes about 100 species of ferns. The family Blechnaceae is a group of ferns commonly known as chain ferns, due to their distinctive chain-like fronds. Blechnum banksii is native to Australia and New Zealand. The classification of Blechnum banksii is further supported by its unique characteristics and morphological features.

intro: Blechnum banksii, also known as the waterfall fern or banks fern, is a type of epiphytic fern native to Australia and New Zealand. The plant is used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions, including inflammation and respiratory issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties. The bioactive constituents of Blechnum banksii include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and sesquiterpenes, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
